bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no need to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer is valid but gives me my lowest value on every issue, so I treat the “balanced” message as cheap talk rather than evidence of fairness.

I targeted a very high utility bid since it is still the opening phase at t=0.02 and the opponent has not conceded, repeating an all-valueC offer that is very poor for me. Their messages describe the offer as fair, but the actual bids show they are holding firm at my lowest values, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while preserving my most important issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to concede much yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueC offer three times despite calling it fair, so their messages are cheap talk and their bidding shows no real concession; I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.961 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueC package that is very poor for me. Their messages claim fairness, but the offer history shows they are holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while preserving my most important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, and the opponent has only made a small concession on issueB while otherwise holding mostly at my worst values. Their “win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual offers remain low for me, so I will concede only on lower-weight issues and hold firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me, despite their small concession on issueB. Their messages frame the offers as fair, but the actual bids are still mostly valueC, so I treat that as cheap talk and concede only on lower-weight issues while holding issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has only made a small concession on issueB while remaining at valueC on all other issues. Their messages describe the offers as fair, but the bid history shows they are still mostly holding firm, so I will concede only on lower-weight issues while preserving valueA on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.883 by holding firm on the three most important issues while conceding to valueB on the lower-weight issues. At t=0.14 it is still early, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility package, though their recent movement on issueA/issueB suggests gradual concession. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual offers remain heavily unfavorable to me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 by holding firm on the important issues A, B, and C while conceding further on a low-weight issue. The opponent’s messages claim balance, but their actual offers remain very low for me with only small concessions, and at t=0.16 there is still little time pressure to make major concessions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84, still holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while rotating concessions on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid. At t=0.18 there is no need for a large concession yet, especially since the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small one-issue moves and “balanced” language. Their history suggests only limited concession so far, so I am offering flexibility on D/E/F while preserving my core value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.80 while making a clearer concession on lower-weight issues to encourage movement. At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C, especially since the opponent’s offers remain low for me despite some movement on issueB. Their messages claim balance, but the actual bids are still mostly valueC, so I trust the offer history more than the wording.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility by holding firm on my highest-weight issues A, B, and C while rotating concessions among lower-weight issues. At t=0.22 there is still time, and although the opponent has moved on issueB, most of their offers remain low-value for me, so I should not make a major concession yet. Their messages claim balance, but the offer history shows only limited movement, so I am offering a modest tradeoff on lower-priority issues.

At t=0.24 it is still early-to-mid negotiation, so I am targeting a solid utility while making visible concessions on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain mostly at valueC, but they have shown movement on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA/issueB/issueC and concede issueD/issueE/issueF where the cost to me is smaller.

At t=0.26 we are still relatively early, so I am targeting a solid utility while making a meaningful concession beyond my prior offer. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their “balanced” messages are not supported by the numbers, but they have repeatedly pushed valueC across many issues, so I concede lower-priority D/E/F and move issueC to valueB while holding my most important issues A and B at valueA.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72, holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B while conceding on lower-weight issues and moving issueC to valueB. At t=0.28 there is no need to rush to their low-utility offers, though their latest bid shows modest movement on issueA and issueC; I treat the “balanced/win-win” messages as cheap talk and rely on the actual bid pattern.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.68: I keep my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA, while conceding low-weight issues D/E/F to valueC and allowing issueC at valueB. At t=0.30 there is still time, but the opponent has mostly held at valueC with only selective concessions, so this bid uses their signaled willingness on issueA/issueB while giving ground where it costs me less.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 because we are only at t=0.32, so I should not rush to the opponent’s much lower offers yet. The opponent’s actual bids show gradual movement on issueA/issueB and occasional movement on issueC/issueF, while their “fair win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding low-weight issueD and issueE.

At t=0.34 I am still targeting a solid utility above 0.65 while showing continued movement on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain mostly unfavorable to me and their “win-win” messages overstate the actual concessions, but they have intermittently moved on issueB, issueA, issueC, and issueF, so I will hold firm on my highest-value issueA/issueB while conceding more on the lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.60 because we are only at t=0.36, so I should concede but not rush to the opponent’s much lower-utility offers. The opponent has shown some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while mostly keeping several issues at valueC; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while conceding on lower-weight issues and partially on issueB/issueC to improve acceptability.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while making concessions on lower-weight issues D/E/F and offering a compromise on C. At t=0.38 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility proposals yet; their offers show intermittent concessions on A and B but continued pressure toward valueC on most issues, so this counter keeps the valuable concessions they have already signaled.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.72 while still showing movement on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, but they have shown occasional concessions on issueA, issueB, issueE, and issueF, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA/issueB and keep a moderate concession on issueC while varying the low-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.68: I keep my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA, while conceding issueC and the lower-weight issueD/issueE toward valueC and matching their recent movement on issueF=valueB. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to their low-utility packages; their messages claim fairness, but the offer history shows only limited and inconsistent concessions.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding substantially on lower-weight issues while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly for me and their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, but their history suggests they often push for valueC, so I am offering valueC on several lower-priority issues and valueB on issueC as a meaningful compromise.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68, which is a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting my highest-weight issues A and B. The opponent’s messages claim balance, but their actual latest offer is still low for me; however, they have signaled willingness on issueD and issueF at valueB, so I match those and concede issueC/issueE to valueC to improve acceptability at t=0.46.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.682, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting my high-weight issues A and B. At t=0.48 there is no need to drop to the opponent’s current offer yet, but their latest bid shows real movement on issueA and issueC, while their “balanced” messages are cheap talk. This counter keeps issueA and issueB at my best values, accepts issueC at valueB, and concedes the lower-weight issues D/E/F to valueC.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.68, holding firm on the highest-weight issues issueA and issueB while conceding more on lower-weight issues and one larger concession on issueC. The opponent’s offers have improved from all valueC to occasional valueA/valueB on issueA, issueB, and issueF, but their “balanced” messages are cheap talk; the numbers show gradual movement but still below what I should accept at t=0.50.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers while still preserving value on the highest-weight issueA. At t=0.52 we are in the mid-game, and the opponent has shown some willingness to move on issueA, issueB, and issueF, while mostly holding issueC/D/E at valueC; their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, but their bid history suggests this package may be within reach.
